perturbation.original_text: 2.1 This Agreement shall continue to be in force for five (5) years period from the date of this Agreement , or until the completion of the Purpose , whichever is later, unless terminated prematurely in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 This Agreement shall continue to be in force for five (5) years period from the date of this Agreement , or until the completion of the Purpose , whichever is later, but in no event shall it exceed three (3) years, unless terminated prematurely in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'but in no event shall it exceed three (3) years' introduces an upper limit on the agreement's duration that contradicts the original clause. The original clause states that the agreement lasts for five years or until the completion of the Purpose, whichever is later, creating a potential In-Text Contradiction with termination clause 2.2 (b), which allows termination with 30 days' notice. If the Purpose is not completed within 3 years, it's unclear if the agreement automatically terminates at 3 years or continues until 5 years if the Purpose is not complete, leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 This Agreement may be terminated: (a) by mutual consent; or (b) by either party giving to the other not less than thirty (30) days’ prior written notice .
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 2.1 introduces a three-year limit, creating a direct contradiction with the original five-year term and the 'completion of the Purpose' clause. This contradicts section 2.2(b), which allows termination with 30 days' notice. The ambiguity arises because it is unclear whether the agreement automatically terminates after three years if the Purpose is not completed, potentially conflicting with the termination clause allowing either party to terminate with 30 days' notice, thus fitting the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 In consideration of the foregoing and the exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information by IHiS , the Company undertakes in relation to IHiS’ Confidential Informatio n: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose;
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 In consideration of the foregoing and the exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information by IHiS , the Company undertakes in relation to IHiS’ Confidential Informatio n: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose, or any other project approved by the Company's legal department, and for no other purpose;
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'or any other project approved by the Company's legal department' to the clause about maintaining confidentiality, it broadens the scope of permitted use of confidential information beyond the defined "Purpose". This creates an In-Text Contradiction with section 9.1, which obligates the Company to keep the existence and nature of the agreement confidential and not to use the name of IHiS without prior written consent. The added phrase makes it unclear whether the Company can use confidential information for projects unrelated to the purpose but related to IHIS without IHIS written approval, potentially allowing disclosure of IHiS information to projects which IHIS has not been given prior concent, leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 The Company agrees to keep the existence and nature of this Agreement confidential and not to use the same or the name of IHiS or of any other Affiliate of IHiS in any publicity, advertisement or other disclosure with regard to this Agreement without the prior written consent of IHiS, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 3.1 broadens the permitted use of confidential information beyond the defined 'Purpose' by adding 'or any other project approved by the Company's legal department'. This directly contradicts section 9.1, which requires the Company to keep the existence and nature of the agreement confidential and not use IHiS's name without prior written consent. The addition in 3.1 allows for potential disclosure of IHiS's confidential information to projects outside the original 'Purpose' without IHiS's written consent, thus creating a clear and direct conflict with the confidentiality requirements outlined in section 9.1.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 The Company shall within seven days of : (f) completion of the Purpose ; or (g) receipt of a written request from IHiS; or (h) expiry or termination of the Agreement, return to IHiS all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the IHiS’ Confidential Information or destroy the same, and certify in writing to IHiS that it has complied with the requirements of this sub -clause .
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 The Company shall within seven days of : (f) completion of the Purpose ; or (g) receipt of a written request from IHiS, which IHiS shall not make unless there is a potential breach of contract ; or (h) expiry or termination of the Agreement, return to IHiS all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the IHiS’ Confidential Information or destroy the same, and certify in writing to IHiS that it has complied with the requirements of this sub -clause .
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'which IHiS shall not make unless there is a potential breach of contract', the ability of IHiS to request the return of confidential information is restricted. It creates an In-Text Contradiction, because under clause 2.2 (b), IHiS has the right to terminate with 30 days notice, regardless of a breach, and according to 5.1 upon termination, the company will return all confidential information to IHIS. But with the new addition, the only was IHIS can make such written request, is when there is a potential breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 This Agreement may be terminated: (a) by mutual consent; or (b) by either party giving to the other not less than thirty (30) days’ prior written notice .
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5.1 adds a restriction on IHiS's ability to request the return of confidential information, limiting it to situations with a potential breach of contract. This directly contradicts section 2.2(b), which allows IHiS to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice regardless of any breach. Upon termination, according to the original 5.1, the company must return all confidential information. Therefore, the amendment to 5.1 creates a direct conflict with 2.2(b) because IHiS might terminate without a breach but would be unable to demand the return of confidential information unless a breach occurred, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 The Company shall render all assistance to IHiS in any action or proceeding and shall indemnify and hold IHiS harmless against all costs, expenses, losses, fees, penalties, charges, damages, claims, actions , proceedings , investigations, complaints, orders, directions and judgements (including legal costs on a full indemnity basis) of whatsoever nature and howsoever incurred and suffered arising out of or in connection with the Company’s breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 The Company shall render all assistance to IHiS in any action or proceeding and shall indemnify and hold IHiS harmless against all costs, expenses, losses, fees, penalties, charges, damages, claims, actions , proceedings , investigations, complaints, orders, directions and judgements (including legal costs on a full indemnity basis) of whatsoever nature and howsoever incurred and suffered arising out of or in connection with the Company’s gross negligence or willful misconduct in the performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the indemnity clause to cover only 'gross negligence or willful misconduct' instead of 'breach of this Agreement', it narrows the scope of indemnification. This creates an In-Text contradiction. Clause 3.2 states, 'The Company as the principal party shall be responsible and held liable for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives'. But the original clause 10.1, stated that The Company shall render all assistance to IHiS in any action or proceeding and shall indemnify and hold IHiS harmless against all costs, expenses, losses, fees, penalties, charges, damages, claims, actions , proceedings , investigations, complaints, orders, directions and judgements (including legal costs on a full indemnity basis) of whatsoever nature and howsoever incurred and suffered arising out of or in connection with the Company’s breach of this Agreement., now under the new modified agreement, what happens, the company's representatives breach the agreemen, but it isnt gross negligence or wilful misconduct?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 The Company shall cause its Representatives involved in the Purpose to observe or be similarly bound by the terms of Agreement. The Company as the principal party shall be responsible and held liable for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives .
perturbation.justification: The changed text in clause 10.1 narrows the scope of indemnification to 'gross negligence or willful misconduct,' whereas the original text covered any 'breach of this Agreement.' This directly contradicts clause 3.2, which states that 'The Company as the principal party shall be responsible and held liable for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.' If a representative breaches the agreement but it does not amount to gross negligence or wilful misconduct, under the modified clause 10.1, IHiS would not be indemnified, creating a direct conflict with the liability stated in clause 3.2. This fits the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.
